fix: push heartbeat user filters into SQL (#1015)

HeartbeatScheduler.tick() loaded the entire users table every 60s and
filtered onboarding_complete/is_active in Python. Move the filters into
the SELECT so dormant rows are never materialized. Savings scale with the
ratio of total users to onboarded-active users, which matters under the
multi-tenant deployment where dormant accounts accumulate.

fix(transcription): fall back to CPU when CUDA runtime libs are missing

faster-whisper's device="auto" picks CUDA when ctranslate2's wheel
ships CUDA shared libs, even on hosts without the NVIDIA runtime
(libcublas.so.12 / libcudnn*). On those hosts the model often loads
fine but transcribe() fails at first dlopen, and the broken model
stays cached in the module-global — every subsequent voice message
in the gateway process fails identically until restart.

- Add _load_local_whisper_model() wra

fix: persist preferred_channel drift sync in resolve_heartbeat_route (#1016)

The heartbeat scheduler loads users on one session, expunges them, then
processes each in a fresh session. resolve_heartbeat_route mutated the
detached User and called db.commit(), but SQLAlchemy did not track the
change so the update never hit the database. Any code that later read
User.preferred_channel from a fresh load would see a stale value.

Attach the user via db.merge before mutating so the commit actually
per

install: revert to tailnet-only — Funnel was the wrong default

Funnel exposes the .ts.net hostname to the public internet via
Tailscale's edge nodes, which terminates TLS at Tailscale and means
any leak of the gateway bearer token (browser history, screenshots
of the QR, server logs that capture URL params) is exploitable from
anywhere on the internet. For a personal AI you talk to from your
own phone, the right model is end-to-end tunnel encryption between
two devices on the same tailnet.

fix(installer): #22 atomic manifest write via temp + rename (#41)

The previous `writeFileSync` was open-write-close — a concurrent
reader (verify.mjs running in parallel, an editor file watcher, or
a launchd-restart racing the install) could observe the file in a
truncated state mid-write and parse-fail with "manifest corrupt."
Worse: a power loss or process kill between the truncate and the
write would leave a permanently-broken manifest.

Fix: temp-then-rename, the standard atomic-write patte

would have gotten a plugi

fix(invite): per-code funnel table headers use tt() instead of data-i18n (#2038)

scanner; dynamically-injected innerHTML isn't re-scanned. Live prod
rendered zh-CN summary + status badge correctly but left CODE / CLICKS /
UNIQUE / STATUS headers as the hardcoded English fallback. Switched to
tt() which resolves the key against the active locale at build time,
matching the summary/badge pattern already in use in loadClickFunne

fix(backend): serialize SQL datetime fields in room previews (#327)

The PostgreSQL get_agent_room_previews() path returned raw datetime
objects for created_at/last_message_at, while the ORM fallback already
called .isoformat(). The new HumanAgentRoomSummary response model
declares these as `str | None`, so Pydantic v2 rejected the datetime
values with a 500 on /api/humans/me/agent-rooms (owner-chat refresh)
in preview/prod. SQLite tests passed because they hit the ORM branch.

fix(frontend): preserve streamed assistant text on owner-chat finalize (#326)

When an agent streams multiple assistant blocks but only sends a short
final message via botcord_send (e.g. Codex reasoning + "Hi."),
finalizeStream was overwriting the accumulated streamed text with the
shorter finalData.text, causing the visible answer to collapse.

Now keep the longer of the two, appending finalText as a suffix when
it is not already contained in the streamed text.

phase, so all groups run concurrently via join_all without SQLite write

 N × single_call.

perf(memory): parallelize per-group summary generation and increase LLM timeout to 60s

Archive groups are independent — each group has its own kind+topic,
source records, and DB writes with no cross-group dependencies. Replace
the serial for-loop with futures_util::future::join_all so all group
summary LLM calls execute concurrently. This reduces total archive time
from N×single_call to max(single_calls), which is critical when there
are multiple stale groups.

fix: recall-weighted overlap scoring for anime fansub titles

The old token_overlap_score used max(|Q|,|T|) as denominator, which
crushed short queries against long fansub titles (2/10 = 0.20, below
the 0.62 threshold).  Switched to adaptive F-beta scoring:
- F2 (recall-heavy) when title has 3x+ more tokens than query
- F1 (balanced) for moderately-sized titles to avoid over-promoting
  mention-only matches

multi-tool turn that kept producing visible progress (tool calls,
streaming text) could hit it even while actively working. Now it's an
idle timeout that resets on every assistant step. Claude has to go
genuinely silent for N seconds for the turn to reject.

The planner was using ListCapabilities() directly, sending ALL tools
to its system prompt regardless of what the RAG preparer returned.
Now it filters capabilities through the same relevant_tools list,
so the planner only sees tools that matched the user's query.

Root cause: FileWatchConnector uses fs.watch() and SystemConnector uses
setInterval — both have afterEach cleanup but fs.watch handles on Linux
sometimes stay open after close(), blocking process exit.

Node 22+ supports --test-force-exit which terminates cleanly after all
tests complete, regardless of dangling handles.

fix: stop infinite SSE reconnect on 401 and add exponential backoff (#1009)

The activity SSE stream (subscribeToActivity) retried every 2 seconds on
any failure, including 401 Unauthorized. When a token expired or a stale
browser tab lost its session, this created an infinite loop of 401s that
spammed server logs and wasted resources.

Two changes:
- Stop retrying on 401/403 (auth errors won't self-heal by reconnecting)
- Use exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, capped at 30s) for transient

fix(opencode): surface provider APIErrors instead of silent _Done_ (#205)

* fix(opencode): surface provider APIErrors instead of silent _Done_

The OpenCode SDK returns HTTP 200 even when the underlying provider call
failed: the failure is stashed on `data.info.error` (e.g. Anthropic
APIError when an image in session history exceeds 2000px) and the
assistant message ends up with zero text parts. The adapter only looked
at the top-level `result.error`, so the kernel silently fell back to
`_Done_

Outlook: skip self-addressed messages to prevent reply loops

When the authenticated user forwards or replies to themselves, the
/reply endpoint sends our Claude-generated reply back to that same
address, which arrives in the subscribed inbox and triggers another
notification. Each round sends an email, ad infinitum.

Fix: fetch /me once, cache it, and short-circuit in handleNotification
when the From: address equals the user's own. Logged at debug level.

Add environment variable augmentation for shell commands

This commit introduces an `AugmentEnv` function to enhance the environment variables for shell commands in both Unix and Windows implementations. The Unix version appends common user bin paths to the `PATH` variable, addressing issues with non-login shells that may not load user-specific directories. The Windows version simply returns the original environment, as user bin paths are managed by system policies. This improvement ensures that

fix(experts): recognize Windows junction links to central store

On Windows the expert enable-status check compared `fs::read_link` output
against the central path, which silently failed for junctions: the link
state fell back to Broken/LinkedElsewhere, so enabled experts kept
reappearing as disabled and disappeared from the message-input expert
list. Switch the link verification to canonicalize both sides (which
transparently follows junctions and symlinks) and add a case-insensitive
path compa

fix(parity): reset retryCounters on enter_plan_mode + plan approval

Matches openclaw-1 semantics where the retry counters lived in
per-attempt memory and were naturally fresh per cycle. The plugin
persists them on session state so they survive across runs, which
means we MUST explicitly reset at the right boundaries:

- enter_plan_mode (fresh planning cycle) → all counters reset
- /plan accept|edit (fresh execution phase) → all counters reset

Without these resets, an ack-only stall in cycle N

fix: use quote=False in html.escape for element content to prevent ' breaking Telegram HTML parse

Telegram's HTML mode only recognises <, >, &, and " — not '.
Any apostrophe in code blocks or text caused Telegram to reject the parse_mode=HTML message,
triggering the fallback that sent raw HTML tags as plain text.

O(N²) traffic through the event pipeline, multi-GB transient allocations
and WKWebView crashes. The fix turns cumulative snapshots into bounded
incremental deltas and removes redundant payload copies.

   fall back to raw text. execSync properly waits for the full output.

fix(llm): backport ea0eda11 — propagate provider_extra (Gemini thought_signature) end-to-end (#294)

When Gemini issues a tool call, the response carries `extra_content`
(`thought_signature`) that must be echoed back on the next turn or
the API rejects subsequent tool messages with HTTP 400.

The `ToolUseBlock.provider_extra` field already exists, but several
links in the message pipeline silently drop it. This patch threads
`provider_extra` through every conversion / reconstruction point so a
m

fix(llm): fix progressive cooldown never reaching higher steps for persistently failing endpoints

Backport of v1.26.x a82f59d1.

Two bugs in the cooldown escalation mechanism:

1. Local endpoint ConnectError (server not running) was exempt from
   progressive escalation. Only Timeout should be exempt, since
   timeout means the server is running but slow. ConnectError means
   the server is completely unreachable and retrying every 5s wastes
   resources. Now only Timeout is exempt via _is_time

because openai-codex aliases to the openai entry there. At 1.05M the
compressor never fires and requests hard-fail with 'context window
exceeded' around the real 272k boundary.

fix(install): set NEMOCLAW_INSTALLING guard before npm link in prepare (#2413)

## Summary

- Fixes fork-bomb caused by `npm link` in `prepare` recursively
re-triggering `prepare` during a bare `npm install`
- Sets `NEMOCLAW_INSTALLING=1` inline so the re-entered `prepare`
short-circuits, breaking the recursion
- One-line change; verified to resolve the 1000+ stuck process
reproduction described in the issue

Closes #2284

每请求都产生这条 flag — false positive.

根因: max-tool-calls-per-task 是 static policy (always applicable=True),
但它的 signal (tool_call_count) 是跨请求聚合值, 不应在单请求 runtime_gate
context 里存在。原逻辑 fall-through 到 "static applicable=True 无 signal
→ flag" 的分支是设计缺陷:
  - static policy 的 applicable=True 仅表示"策略适用于所有请求"(恒等),
    不是"触发告警"
  - 真正

fix(llm): preserve content-safety error info end-to-end and avoid prompt-triggered moderation

DashScope (and other Chinese cloud LLMs) reject requests when input matches
their safety filter, returning HTTP 400 with `data_inspection_failed`. Two
defects caused chat to silently break with a misleading "check API key"
message:

fix(chat): disable streaming-recovery GET that was truncating long replies

The recovery logic fetched /v1/sessions/<chatId>/history on load and
overwrote any _streaming message with it. But the chat frontend does not
route through gateway sessions, so the returned content was unrelated
short messages from other sessions — causing long assistant replies to
be truncated to the first chunk after any refresh.

Now we simply clear _streaming flag on load without overwriting content.

fix(chat): upload dragged documents instead of inlining base64 in prompt

The Tauri native drag-drop path attached every file as a base64 data URL
on attachment.url, including .docx / .pdf / .xlsx and other documents.
The backend Desktop attachment handler then concatenated that URL into
the LLM prompt text (e.g. "[file: foo.docx (...)] URL: data:...;base64,..."),
which exploded the token budget and was truncated to "..." by intermediate
layers, so the model kept replying that it could not find

fresh-install branch — but it was still inside the
SKIP_OPENCLAW_SETUP wrapper that gates Steps 1-2. Users who
already had openclaw installed and chose "keep my OpenClaw
intact" went down the skip path, so the /etc/profile.d write
never fired and `openclaw: command not found` persisted in
fresh shells.

Move the call OUTSIDE the SKIP wrapper so persistence runs
on every install path

chore(daemon): drop stale package-lock.json to fix publish workflow (#299)

The lockfile is missing optional platform binaries (lightningcss-*,
@rolldown/binding-*) pulled in by vitest@^4, causing `npm ci` to fail
with EUSAGE in the Publish Package workflow. The workflow already
falls back to `npm install` when no lockfile is present, which is
sufficient for daemon's simple dep tree.

/etc/environment).

unconditionally after the install/skip dust settles. Idempotent:
guarded by grep -qF and grep -q on

feat(plugins): optional match predicate on register_hook

Plugins can now pass match=lambda **kwargs: bool to register_hook so the
dispatcher skips irrelevant events without paying the per-callback timeout
and exception-isolation cost. The predicate is stored on the callback
object via __hook_match__ and evaluated inside both the async dispatch
(_run_one) and the sync dispatch loop, including the wrapped path used for
callables that reject arbitrary setattr (e.g. builtins, __slots__ objects).

fix(worker): clamp MNEMOS_CONTEST_STALE_THRESHOLD_SECS to non-negative

Codex post-audit of the v3.1.1 stranded-running sweep (68615e9)
flagged the env-var parsing path as a footgun: a negative value
turns the sweep cutoff into NOW() + |t|, which makes every non-NULL
running row qualify as stale immediately — a silent queue wipe on
the first batch drain.

Replace the bare int() with _parse_stale_threshold_secs():
  - ValueError on non-integer input -> warn + return 0 (sweep off)
  - negative int

feat(helpqueue): DM every mentor when a new ticket is posted

Role pings in the queue channel depend on each mentor's role-mention
notification settings — many mentors miss them, especially when in
Focus Mode or with mentions muted. Now we also fan out a direct
message to every member of the matching mentor role whenever a ticket
is created. Mentors who block DMs from server members just don't
receive the DM; the queue-channel embed is still authoritative.

The cat > /etc/profile.d/openclaw.sh line and the parallel
echo >> /etc/environment / cat >> /etc/bash.bashrc lines were writing
to root-owned system files directly, with no $SUDO wrapper. On a
non-root install (Ubuntu user with sudo), these silently failed
under errexit and aborted the whole installer with "Permission
denied" right after the npm install of openclaw.

Wrapping each system-file write in $SUDO tee makes the pat

Sandbox users reported that basic process-inspection commands were
missing:

```

bash: ps: command not found
```

Without `ps` it is very hard to observe what is running inside the
container. `procps` is the standard Debian package th

fix(daemon): drop stale runtime session when resume fails (#295)

* fix(daemon): drop stale runtime session when resume fails

Claude Code emits a fresh `session_id` even on a non-success result
(e.g. `--resume <missing-uuid>`), so the dispatcher was persisting a
useless UUID and re-resuming it every turn. The adapter now wipes
`newSessionId` on non-success results, and the dispatcher deletes the
entry when a turn errors with no surviving session id.

Also adds docs/daemon-runtime-session-mappin
feat(dashboard): implement contact management features including contact removal and modals for human interactions

feat(gateway): registry-backed provider resolution (v3.2)

Codex memory-OS audit 019dbd11 flagged: "Gateway provider selection
is still heuristic and brittle: substring routing plus a default-to-
Groq fallback." Pre-v3.2, `/v1/chat/completions` with any unknown
model_id silently routed to Groq. Typos, new frontier models, and
custom deployments all got misrouted.

v3.2 resolves the provider from `model_registry` first; heuristic
substring match is a last-resort fallback only.

Phase 4: no restart needed, clarify journalctl is for systemd only

fix: migrate Langfuse integration from start_generation to start_obse… (#14205)

The Langfuse Python SDK v3+ removed `start_generation()` method.
RagFlow's code called this non-existent method, causing AttributeError
when Langfuse tracing is enabled.

Replace all `start_generation()` calls with
`start_observation(as_type="generation")` which is the correct v4 SDK
API.

- api/db/services/llm_service.py (12 occurrences)
- api/db/services/dialog_service.py (1 occurrence)

funnel events actually reach PostHog.

fix(chat): bust browser cache for note-link-render.js fix (#2020)

PR #2019 fixed the note-chip ellipsis regex in note-link-render.js,
but chat.html loads the script without a version query string, so
existing users' browsers keep serving the pre-fix bundle from disk
cache until the 4-hr Cloudflare max-age expires (page-rule overrides
our server-side max-age=60 to max-age=14400 on prod).

Append ?v=0e95a36c (the PR #2019 merge SHA) to force a fresh fetch
and roll the chip fix out immediately.

HALF_OPEN in 9bf1813, the Codex review flagged a stale-probe race:
if a probe caller crashed without recording a result, auto-admitting
a replacement probe would be unsafe — a late completion from the
abandoned probe could pollute the replacement's state. v3.1.1
punted: no auto-replacement, operators recover via reset().

with what `openshell inference get` reported.

fix(ci): enable NVIDIA self-hosted runners for PR E2E tests (#2374)

## Summary

Fix snapshot rollback failure on NVIDIA self-hosted runners and move all
E2E jobs back to self-hosted.

## Root Cause

NVIDIA self-hosted runners use Docker with the **containerd overlayfs
snapshotter** (`io.containerd.snapshotter.v1`), while GitHub-hosted
runners use the legacy **`overlay2`** driver. On the containerd
snapshotter, directories can span different overlay layers, causing
`rename(2)` to return **`EXDEV

fix(hermes): ensure sandbox user can read blueprint files (#2329)

## Summary

`COPY nemoclaw-blueprint/ /opt/nemoclaw-blueprint/` places files owned
by root. When the Dockerfile subsequently switches to `USER sandbox` and
runs `cp -r /opt/nemoclaw-blueprint/*`, the sandbox user cannot read
those files on systems where Docker's default umask results in
restrictive permissions (e.g. `640` or tighter).

Add `RUN chmod -R a+rX /opt/nemoclaw-blueprint/` immediately after the
`COPY` step to explicitl

chore(repo): stop tracking CLAUDE.md (gitignored dev-aid)

CLAUDE.md was added to .gitignore in this tree but the file remained
tracked from prior commits — so every local edit still surfaced in
`git status` and could accidentally be swept into a commit. `git rm
--cached` removes it from the index while leaving the working-tree
copy intact, so the gitignore entry actually takes effect going
forward.

Net effect:

  - Index: CLAU

per call.

for-raw-in-sys.stdin — i.e., a background thread (debug print to stderr,
most likely from HERMES_VOICE_DEBUG=1) wrote to a pipe whose buffer the
TUI hadn't drained yet, and SIG_DFL promptly killed the process.

- entry.py: SIGPIPE → SIG_IGN

fix(invite-tracker): reload mapping on every join

Rollout scripts write new invite→role mappings straight to the JSON
file on disk, but the tracker only loaded it at __init__ — so any
invite added after bot startup was "unmapped" in memory, causing
single-use joins to fall through as unidentified. Now the mapping
is re-read on each used_code_for_member call (cheap — the file is
tiny and membership joins are infrequent).

recreate when non-patchable metadata differs, POST missing, and delete
stale commands one-by-one. Keeps 'bulk' for legacy behavior and 'off'
to skip startup sync entirely.

feat(agent): make API retry count configurable via agent.api_max_retries (#14730)

Closes #11616.

The agent's API retry loop hardcoded max_retries = 3, so users with
fallback providers on flaky primaries burned through ~3 × provider
timeout (e.g. 3 × 180s = 9 minutes) before their fallback chain got a
chance to kick in.

Expose a new config key:

    agent:
      api_max_retries: 3  # default unchanged

Set it to 1 for fast failover when you have fallback providers, or
raise it if you prefer lo

fix(gateway): kill tool subprocesses before adapter disconnect on drain timeout (#14728)

Closes #8202.

Root cause: stop() reclaimed tool-call bash/sleep children only at the
very end of the shutdown sequence — after a 60s drain, 5s interrupt
grace, and per-adapter disconnect. Under systemd (TimeoutStopSec bounded
by drain_timeout), that meant the cgroup SIGKILL escalation fired first,
and systemd reaped the bash/sleep children instead of us.

- Extract tool-subprocess cleanup into a local

ci(nightly-e2e): scope concurrency group by event and branch (#2379)

## Summary

The nightly-e2e workflow used a flat concurrency group (`nightly-e2e`)
that let `workflow_dispatch` runs cancel the scheduled nightly cron and
vice versa. The last two consecutive nightly cron runs (Apr 22–23) were
both cancelled by dispatch runs on `fix/slack-auth-crash-2340`, leaving
no nightly data since Apr 21.

## Related Issue

Fixes #2375

feat(runtime): add rotate_session() for long-running agents

Bounded-memory primitive for 24/7 service agents. Pre-existing reset()
was the only way to trim the monotonic growth of trace.events,
_atom_caches, _turn_spans, _log, and _violations — but reset() reads
as "emergency start over" and returns nothing, so ops couldn't audit
what was flushed during periodic housekeeping.

  violations_cleared=0}: thread-safe wrapper over res
